response.justification: Candidate is a strong full-stack software engineer with web, mobile, and some embedded background, but the role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, performance architecture, and deep learning workload optimization — none of which appear on the resume. No GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture experience, no hardware-software co-design background, and no advanced degree in a relevant engineering field. The gap between profile and requirements is too wide for a Principal Performance Architect role.
strengths: Long track record (15+ years) of professional software engineering across multiple stacks
Some low-level/high-performance work in DLang at Sociomantic (low-latency, async I/O, fibers)
C/C++ exposure at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or hardware architecture experience
No GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or deep learning training/inference optimization background
Educational path (BS only) without the years of relevant hardware/performance experience required

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC hardware expertise
No deep learning workload or GPU architecture experience
Some C/C++ and low-latency systems exposure
